title: Schools of Thought
editorialSummary: Western philosophical schools sorted by their core commitment: rationalist (Descartes, Leibniz, Spinoza, Wolff), empiricist (Locke, Hume, Berkeley, Bacon), existentialist (Sartre, Camus, Heidegger, de Beauvoir), and utilitarian (Bentham, Mill, Sidgwick, Smart). All philosophers are textbook representatives of exactly one school.

meta.gptRationale: The philosopher names are canonical but still fairly academic, and assigning each thinker to the correct school requires real humanities knowledge.
